description_long: This is a tall, stone obelisk monument with a square base and a tapering shaft that culminates in a cross at the tip. The base is multi-tiered, featuring several steps and a prominent plinth, with visible weathering and moss growth along the lower sections. One face of the obelisk displays engraved plaques and inscriptions, likely commemorative or memorial in nature, while the other sides are plainer but show signs of aging and discoloration. The structure is constructed from light-colored stone, possibly limestone or granite, and exhibits a rough, matte finish with some chipped and eroded areas. The monument is solid and immovable, designed to serve as a permanent fixture in a public or park setting. Decorative elements are minimal, focusing on the cross at the top and the engraved panels on the front.
description: A tall, weathered stone obelisk with a cross at the tip, set on a multi-tiered square base with engraved plaques and visible moss growth.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cross-topped shaft, multi-stepped base, and some moss and weathering on the lower section.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tapering shaft, plain stone surface, and more pronounced moss and discoloration on the base.
description_view_2: Rear view with visible engraved plaques and inscriptions on the base, as well as additional weathering and chipped areas.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the plain shaft, stepped base, and significant erosion or damage on the lower right corner.
attribute_inscription: Engraved plaques and inscriptions are visible on the front face of the base, likely in English, with block lettering. The text appears weathered but partially legible.
attribute_ornamentation: A cross is present at the tip, serving as the primary decorative element. No other significant ornamentation is visible.
attribute_base_design: The base is square and multi-tiered, with several steps leading up to a prominent plinth. The lower sections show moss growth and weathering, and the front face features engraved plaques.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is capped with a stone cross, which is simple in design and matches the material and finish of the obelisk.
